name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) is trivially true for all addr, zero-size alignment checks would silently pass, masking invalid frame sizes

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_2_inv_allows_empty_arch(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: An architecture with zero layers is degenerate and should not satisfy the invariant, as it cannot describe any address translation

name: upper_vaddr_leq_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_leq_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        base > 0,
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) <= base,
{
}
reason: upper_vaddr should always exceed base for a valid layer, since the mapped region has positive size; equality or less implies an empty or wrapping address space

name: entry_base_zero_idx_neq_base
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_zero_idx_neq_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: entry_base at index 0 should equal the base itself (base + 0 * entry_size == base); if this proves, the arithmetic identity is broken

name: entry_size_next_layer_equal
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_next_layer_equal(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) == arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: Consecutive layers having equal entry sizes would mean num_entries is 1, collapsing the page table hierarchy and defeating multi-level translation
